content: Hey, I'm so excited for my sister's wedding next month in Paris. I've been practicing my French to impress her fiancé's family.
That sounds like a wonderful occasion! What's your sister's fiancé's name, and have you met him before?

His name is Pierre, and we met last year when I visited my sister in Lyon. He's a great guy, and I'm sure they'll be happy together. By the way, I've been thinking of taking my beekeeping hobby to the next level and starting a small business.
That's fascinating! Beekeeping is such a unique hobby. What inspired you to start beekeeping, and how do you plan to balance it with your work as a Pastry Chef?

I started beekeeping about 5 years ago, and it's been a great way to relax and connect with nature. As for balancing work and hobby, I'm lucky to have a supportive team at the Michelin-starred restaurant where I work. They're always willing to lend a hand when I need to take care of my bees.
It's great to have a supportive team. What's the name of the restaurant where you work, and have you ever thought of incorporating your beekeeping skills into your pastry creations?

The restaurant is called Le Coeur de la Vie, and actually, I've been experimenting with using honey from my bees in some of our desserts. It's been a hit with our customers! I'm also excited to share my beekeeping experiences on my new YouTube channel, which I just launched last week.

That sounds like a fantastic idea! I'm sure your YouTube channel will be a great way to share your passions with a wider audience. What's the name of your channel, and what kind of content can viewers expect to see?

My channel is called 'Sweet Treats and Busy Bees,' and I'll be posting videos about my beekeeping adventures, pastry recipes, and maybe even some triathlon training tips. I'm planning to post at least 2 videos per week, starting from March 15th.

I'll have to check it out! It's great that you're pursuing your passions and sharing them with others. How do you plan to manage your time between working at the restaurant, creating content for your channel, and training for triathlons?

It's definitely a challenge, but I'm a bit of a planner, so I've created a schedule that works for me. I wake up at 5 am every day to fit in a workout or some beekeeping tasks before heading to the restaurant. My partner, Alex, is also very supportive and helps me with editing my videos.
That's impressive! It's great that you have a supportive partner. How did you and Alex meet, and what does he think about your new YouTube channel?

We met through mutual friends about 3 years ago, and he's been a huge help with my channel. He's actually the one who encouraged me to start it in the first place. He's actually a big fan of board games, and we love playing together on our free Sundays.

That's wonderful! It's great that you have a shared interest in board games. What's your favorite board game to play together, and have you ever thought of hosting a board game night at the restaurant?

We love playing Scrabble, and that's a great idea about hosting a board game night! I'll have to suggest it to my team. Maybe we can even have a beekeeping-themed game night and raise some money for a local bee conservation organization. We could also have a pottery class as a prize for the winners, since I've been getting into pottery lately and love it.
